claim: Subagents in GitHub Copilot are independent workers that each get their own task, context window, and run in parallel with the main agent
actionable: Use subagents when you need parallel task execution across different domains; each gets a clean context window to prevent interference
source_title: Introduction to Sub Agents in GitHub Copilot | Multi-Agent Workflows

claim: Subagents receive clean context windows without conversation history or prior research noise, returning only final output to main agent
actionable: Delegate research-heavy subtasks to subagents to keep main agent context focused on synthesis and decision-making
source_title: Introduction to Sub Agents in GitHub Copilot | Multi-Agent Workflows

claim: Multiple parallel subagents can each focus on one code review perspective (security, performance, quality) without context pollution from other domains
actionable: Spawn parallel subagents for multi-dimensional review tasks where each agent can focus on one evaluation criterion
source_title: Introduction to Sub Agents in GitHub Copilot | Multi-Agent Workflows

claim: Subagent batches in Copilot are all-or-nothing: the main agent waits for all subagents to complete before continuing; no nested subagents allowed
actionable: Design workflows with a single level of subagent delegation; batch all parallel work in one level rather than nested hierarchies
source_title: Introduction to Sub Agents in GitHub Copilot | Multi-Agent Workflows
